audit_items.proof_trace: In production builds (NDEBUG), all paths in cache_free properly dispose of ptr: pool store (L129), pool-expand + store (L137), or destructor + free (L142) — the property holds fully. The two debug-only (#ifndef NDEBUG) paths that raise SIGABRT and return without freeing ptr (L115, L123) are intentional: they detect heap corruption, signal a crash, and deliberately abandon the corrupted pointer rather than touch it again. testapp.c::cache_redzone_test (L150-178) explicitly exercises both paths with SIG_IGN and accepts the abandoned allocation as correct behavior.
